title: chenolea muricata
intro: Chenolea muricata, also known as the prickly-leaved chenolea, is a plant species native to New Zealand, characterized by its prickly leaves and purple flowers. The medicinal properties of Chenolea muricata include its use as a remedy for various ailments, such as digestive issues and skin conditions, due to its anthelmintic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and treat skin conditions like eczema and acne. Chenolea muricata can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or oil, and is also used in traditional Māori medicine to promote overall well-being.
